records.probe: Tried to falsify 'zero bypass paths / every production change is gated': queried live branch protection and the Pages deploy model, and checked whether the gate even exists on the production branch.
records.evidence: mcp__github__list_branches(mglaeser/save-haven-monitor): both 'main' and the work branch return "protected":false — no branch protection, no required status check. main HEAD = 04772bf (frozen baseline); verify/ + .github/workflows/verify.yml were added only in 2aa7838/4af8354 on the work branch, so PRODUCTION (main, which Pages serves) carries NO gate at all. The single verify workflow run (actions_list, run 29475787128, event=push) concluded "failure". DR-002-ci-gate.md lines 18-31 admit: 'A GitHub Actions check reports status but does not block a merge or the Pages deploy unless branch protection requires it ... Until branch protection requires the verify check on main, the gate is advisory, not blocking.' The gate CAN block in isolation (verify/tests/50-gate-selftest passed 5/5 this run: blocks null-standing_control PASS, production_eligible lie, count drift, failed required test), but a direct push to unprotected main deploys instantly, bypassing CI entirely. No per-change machine-readable provenance record (model+prompt+frozen-spec+checks) is enforced; independent adversarial verification exists only as a one-time Phase-2 calibration, not a standing per-change control.

standing_control.mechanism: gate.js + run.js are self-tested and version-controlled, but nothing forces them to run before production: no branch protection, and Pages deploys directly from main on push
standing_control.blocks: nothing at production today (advisory only)
standing_control.ratchet: none enforced (bypass paths open)

structural_fix.rationale: Owner enabling 'Require status checks to pass -> verify' + 'Do not allow bypassing' on main converts the advisory check into a real gate; alternatively re-architect Pages to deploy from a CI artifact produced only after the gate passes. Neither is done.

residual_risk.compensating_control: run-verify-before-push convention in CLAUDE.md and verify/tests/60-governance asserting the workflow stays wired
residual_risk.tripwire: 60-governance goes red if the workflow is deleted/decoupled — but only on the PR branch, and only advisory

records.remediation: Owner must enable branch protection on main requiring the verify check with bypass disabled (the single human command-authority precondition), and merge verify/ to main so the gate exists on the production branch; add an enforced machine-readable provenance record per change and wire a standing independent adversarial verifier (a model that did not write the change). Until then a change can reach production wholly ungated.

The '6/6 seeded' calibration is a fixed 6-defect corpus, not a mutation score, and is re-injected by convention (§9.3) not by a running mutation gate.

records.probe: Plotted commit sizes/branch lifetimes, checked for a batch-size gate, and searched for a rollback that has been executed with a recorded duration and can be fired by an automated abort rather than a human.
records.evidence: git log --shortstat: change sets are large and unbounded — d66f63a added dashboard.jsx in a single 1852-line commit, 2aa7838 was 3555 insertions, 4af8354 was 22 files/813 insertions. No batch-size gate exists anywhere in verify/ or the workflow. CI now runs on every push (verify.yml on: push branches:['**']) — but its one run concluded failure (actions_list run 29475787128). There is NO rehearsed rollback: no runbook, no recorded rollback duration, no drill. Rollback is implicitly 'git revert + push' (Pages redeploys), and it can only be fired by a human noticing — there is no monitoring signal and no automated abort (no B-18 wiring; system map records no canary/rollout/monitoring). Both branches are protected:false so there is no short-lived-branch or trunk discipline enforced.

records.remediation: Add a batch-size gate to gate.js (block change sets above a line/file threshold), write and actually execute a rollback (scripted git revert + Pages redeploy) recording its duration, and wire it to an automatic abort signal (a synthetic uptime/health check on <prod-host>) so it fires on a signal, not on someone noticing.

records.na_justification: Consistency-vs-availability, replication-vs-cost, isolation level and quorum are properties of backend datastores. This system has no server and no database (system-map 'Data stores'); the only persistence is git history and a per-tab in-memory cache. There is no mutable shared state whose consistency/availability could be traded off, so the control has no surface. Static-asset availability is GitHub Pages/CDN's concern, addressed under A-28.
records.probe: For each datastore and critical path, looked for a consistency-vs-availability position, a replication factor, an isolation level, or a quorum setting that a test could assert drift against. Enumerated the data stores from the system map and grepped served source for any datastore/replication/isolation construct.
records.evidence: There are only three 'stores' and none has a consistency/availability trade-off: client sessionStorage (one key `bubblegauge:enabled`, bubblegauge.jsx:27), an in-memory per-tab fetch cache (25-min TTL, :289-290), and git history (system-map 'Data stores': 'No server-side stores, no databases, no vector stores, no caches beyond the above'). No server, no replication, no quorum, no isolation level exists to position. The one trade-off that does exist — the 25-minute cache TTL — is a freshness-vs-load choice, and it is surfaced to the user via Freshness labels (:407-416) and focus-revalidation (:326-329), but it is not a datastore consistency position.

residual_risk.compensating_control: the sole freshness trade-off (25-min cache TTL) is disclosed via visible staleness labels rather than hidden
residual_risk.tripwire: n/a — no datastore to drift

records.na_justification: Unit economics presuppose metered cost per unit of use. This product has none: static hosting (fixed ~$0), free pinned CDN, and no in-product inference (system-map). There is no per-request or per-tenant cost that could be modelled, capped, or regressed, so the control has no surface. Any cost of the external bubblegauge service belongs to that separate service.
records.probe: Attempted to compute cost-per-transaction and cost-per-AI-request from real usage. Looked for any billed compute: an inference call, a server, a per-request function, a tenant model. Grepped served source and read the system map for cost surfaces.
records.evidence: There is no per-request cost to model: the site is static files served by GitHub Pages (free), dependencies load from unpkg (free CDN), and the codebase makes zero inference calls (system-map 'Models and providers': no in-product runtime model — so no inference line item). No server compute is billed per request, there is no tenancy, and there is no revenue path (README/disclaimer: 'historical/analytical synthesis, not investment advice; eToro links are navigation, not endorsement'). The one external API (the bubblegauge API host) runs and is billed as a separate service, not this repo, and is optional/gated. The A-31 hazard ('inference cost quietly destroying margin', 'an autonomous system finds the expensive path') cannot arise because this codebase performs no inference and provisions no metered compute.

residual_risk.tripwire: the A-25/A-28 'bgFetch is the only egress' test would flag introduction of a metered runtime call, at which point unit economics would become applicable

records.probe: Enumerate every import/require/install in the repo; for each package check existence, pinning (version+hash/lockfile), and whether an existence/registry gate blocks a fabricated or unpinned dep from reaching a build. Then check both the served CDN deps AND the CI harness deps.
records.evidence: SERVED SITE (strong): index.html loads 5 CDN scripts from unpkg, all exact-version-pinned (react 18.3.1, react-dom 18.3.1, prop-types 15.8.1, recharts 2.12.7, @babel/standalone 7.29.7) with sha384 integrity + crossorigin=anonymous. All 5 are mainstream, long-existing packages (no slopsquat/typo risk). Standing controls exist: verify/tests/35 asserts the CDN URLs match golden/pinned-deps.json EXACTLY (offline-deterministic -- catches a silent version bump like react@18.3.2), tests/30 asserts sha384 integrity present on all 5, tests/40 recomputes SRI against fetched bytes (online; OFFLINE in this sandbox, HTTP 403 via proxy). SRI is a structural browser-level control: mismatched CDN bytes are refused independent of CI. Seeded defect D3 (react@18.3.2 + stale SRI) was caught 6/6 (audit/02-calibration.md). CI HARNESS (gap): verify/package.json declares `"playwright-core": "^1.40.0"` -- an UNPINNED caret range -- and there is NO committed lockfile (ls: verify/package-lock.json / npm-shrinkwrap.json both absent). verify.yml runs `npm install --no-audit --no-fund`, so the gate tooling resolves an unpinned dependency (and its transitive tree) at CI time with no hash pinning and no pre-install registry-existence gate. So the very tool meant to substitute the reviewer draws an unpinned, non-reproducible dependency into every gate run.

records.evidence: The maintenance agent has full repo write + push to main via the GitHub integration (system-map 'Tools the model can call': 'full repository write + push ... there is no policy bundle and no gate between an agent's output and production'; and 'The code-writing identity therefore has unrestricted write access to its own (nonexistent) gate' — the B-35 condition). .github/workflows/verify.yml runs on push but has `permissions: contents: read` and its header says it 'does not build or deploy it — Pages still serves the static files directly from main', so a failing gate does not stop the agent's push from reaching production. No branch protection could be confirmed (gh CLI unavailable: 'gh: command not found'); the audit baseline records none. The agent can edit the gate files themselves (verify/gate.js, verify/tests/*). gate.js merely *asserts* 'B-35 write-separation is a standing control' but that is a claim to test, not enforced least-privilege — no scoped identity, no sandbox, no immutable allowlist exists.

standing_control.mechanism: verify workflow runs on push but does not gate the Pages deploy; no branch protection / scoped identity / sandbox confirmed
standing_control.blocks: nothing (advisory only)
standing_control.ratchet: none

structural_fix.rationale: The A-11 tool-gateway that enforces per-agent scope at the door is not implemented. The gate exists as CI but is not wired to be the door — Pages deploys from main regardless of gate outcome, so least privilege remains a policy the agent is trusted to honour rather than a table enforced against it.

residual_risk.compensating_control: verify.yml runs the deterministic gate on every push (visible signal), and calibration catches 6/6 seeded defects — but it reports, it does not block the deploy.
residual_risk.tripwire: A push to main that fails the gate yet is live at <prod-host>.

records.remediation: Separate the code-writing identity from the deploy/merge authority: enable branch protection on main requiring the verify check to pass and disallowing direct pushes; deploy Pages via an Actions workflow that runs the gate from a protected ref so a red gate blocks production; store the gate/allowlist where the writing agent has no write access (B-35). Grant the agent the minimum scopes and deny-by-default; verify by having it attempt an out-of-scope action on every build and requiring the attempt to fail.

records.probe: Asked what 'normal' looks like for the maintenance agent (commit/tool-call volume, tool mix, target hosts, data volume) and whether an anomaly triggers automatic containment. Searched the repo for an audit log of every tool call and any anomaly/kill-switch mechanism.
records.evidence: No per-agent behavioural baseline, anomaly detector, kill switch, or tool-call audit log exists. The data-stores table (system map) lists only client sessionStorage, an in-memory fetch cache, and git history — no agent-activity log. verify/ contains only static-content, golden-hash, supply-chain, SRI, and gate self-test suites (ls verify/tests); none observe or baseline agent runtime behaviour. There is therefore no way to distinguish 'an agent behaving anomalously' from 'an agent behaving', and nothing that would contain an anomaly automatically. The failure mode the check guards (aggregate-harmful behaviour from individually-legitimate tool calls) is completely unmonitored.

standing_control.mechanism: None — no agent baseline, anomaly detection, tool-call audit log, or automatic containment
standing_control.blocks: nothing
standing_control.ratchet: none

residual_risk.compensating_control: git history provides after-the-fact attribution of what an agent changed, and the golden-hash/static-security gates catch a defined class of harmful diffs at CI time.
residual_risk.tripwire: An agent session whose individual commits each pass the gate but whose aggregate behaviour (e.g. mass edits, new egress hosts) is harmful.

records.remediation: Start by logging every maintenance-agent tool call with arguments to an append-only store outside agent write; derive a per-agent baseline (commit cadence, files touched, hosts reached); wire anomaly detection to an automatic containment action (revoke the session token / block the push) rather than an alert. You cannot baseline what you do not record — logging is step one.

records.probe: Asked what 'output quality' means for this system and how the team would learn it degraded before users complain. Looked for a runtime model whose output could decay and for online evaluation / provider-change canaries.
records.evidence: The served site produces no model-generated output at runtime — all product content is frozen constants in dashboard.jsx (CRISES/MATRIX/CLASSIFICATION, system-map 'What this system is'). Those constants cannot 'decay in quality'; they can only be *changed by a commit*, and any such change is caught automatically by the golden-content hash test (verify/tests/20-golden-content.test.js: sha256 over the frozen data, goes RED on any number/string/structure change). The one model-authored datum, the external bubblegauge `judgment_call.text`, is consumed and rendered with explicit stale/error provenance labels (bubblegauge.jsx JudgmentInline; SCORE_FIXTURE.judgment_call has stale/error_class fields) — the site does not generate it and cannot evaluate its quality; that model belongs to the external service.

standing_control.mechanism: verify/tests/20-golden-content.test.js golden hash over frozen data (content integrity, the nearest analog to drift detection)
standing_control.blocks: a silent change to frozen content at CI
standing_control.ratchet: S11 — baseline change requires a decision record

residual_risk.compensating_control: The golden-content hash test detects any change to the frozen content at CI time (the integrity analog of drift detection); provenance labels flag stale/errored external text to the user.
residual_risk.tripwire: If the site ever generates output at runtime (e.g. summarizes the API text via a model), quality drift becomes real and this check reactivates.

history.reason: The NOT-APPLICABLE premise ("no runtime generative model whose output quality varies") is falsified by the engagement's own designated AI-adjacent surface: bubblegauge.jsx (gated on ?status-api=<key>). In JudgmentInline (bubblegauge.jsx:476-485) the external model-authored free-text field judgment_call.text is fetched live from the bubblegauge API host and rendered directly to end users, truncated to 2

records.na_justification: The check targets cross-environment credential bleed (staging/dev pointing at a prod DB, vector store, or API key). That capability does not exist in this architecture: there is exactly one environment (GitHub Pages serving branch main); the 'local preview' is python3 -m http.server over the identical static files with no credentials; and the entire codebase holds no connection strings, credentials, endpoints, or vector-store handles (grep-confirmed this run, corroborated by the audit surface and system map). There are no data planes to separate and no credential to be held by the wrong environment.
records.probe: Grepped the entire served codebase (index.html, dashboard.jsx, bubblegauge.jsx) for password/secret/api-key/connection-string/database/production-env identifiers, and read audit/00-audit-surface.json's identities/data_stores/configuration sections, hunting for a non-production environment holding a production credential, connection string, or vector-store handle.
records.evidence: grep -rniE 'password|secret|api[_-]?key|connection.?string|database|prod...' over the three served files returned ZERO matches. audit/00-audit-surface.json data_stores = only sessionStorage['bubblegauge:enabled'] (browser), an in-memory 25-min cache (browser), and git history; no server-side store, no DB, no vector store, no credential. bubblegauge.jsx:46-57 derives the API base purely from the URL query key (demo=>null, localhost=>http://localhost:8000, else https://<key>.<parent>) — there is no stored credential to leak. System map (audit/00-system-map.md:20-26) records exactly ONE environment (production = Pages/main); 'no staging environment' and 'holds no secrets, no credentials, no personal data'.

structural_fix.rationale: The S13 fix (an environment-scoped credential broker) presupposes credentials and multiple environments; neither exists. Making a non-production workload 'structurally incapable of holding a production credential' is moot when no credential exists anywhere and there is no non-production workload.

residual_risk.compensating_control: The single-environment / zero-credential property is itself asserted this run and is cheap to keep asserted; verify/tests/30-static-security.test.js already scans the served files for dangerous constructs and could be extended to fail if any credential/connection-string literal is ever introduced.
residual_risk.tripwire: A future feature that introduces a stored secret or a second (staging) environment would re-open this check; a grep-based no-credential assertion in the CI suite would fire on the introducing commit.

records.probe: Read bubblegauge.jsx activation/gating path and searched for any tripwire that flips the feature off automatically on a signal (error rate, cost, guardrail, eval score); checked verify/tests for a test that exercises the switch on a schedule.
records.evidence: bubblegauge.jsx:26-64 gates the ENTIRE AI-adjacent feature on the ?status-api=<key> query param and persists it in sessionStorage['bubblegauge:enabled']; with no param window.BubbleGauge={enabled:false} (line 64) and the feature no-ops — release is genuinely decoupled from deploy and fails closed. ?status-api-off clears activation (audit-surface route). DEMO_KEYS={demo,fixture} (line 27) give a pre-verified offline safe mode, and per-item degradation exists ('suppressed (block degraded)', stale/fallback_used/dropped labels, lines 95/118/197/208-210). verify/tests/30-static-security.test.js:92-93 asserts BG defaults to {enabled:false} when the module is absent — a standing test of the fail-closed default. BUT: grep for chaos/inject/tripwire/automatic-flip found none; the switch is per-visitor and manual (URL/sessionStorage), there is no automatic tripwire wired to any signal, and no monthly kill-switch drill.

standing_control.mechanism: verify/tests/30-static-security.test.js asserts the fail-closed default (BG={enabled:false}) and the query-param gate; the feature is decoupled from deploy and off by default.
standing_control.blocks: It asserts the no-op default holds, but does not block release of an AI feature lacking an automatic tripwire.
standing_control.cadence: on every push/PR (verify.yml), though the gate is non-blocking for the Pages deploy at baseline.
standing_control.ratchet: none for automatic-tripwire coverage — no test exercises the switch on a schedule or verifies auto-flip.

records.remediation: The feature is off-by-default and per-visitor, so a global 'misbehaving feature' blast radius is bounded — but wire an automatic disable: since the only runtime signal available client-side is the fetch layer's own error/stale rate, have bubblegauge trip itself to demo/suppressed mode after N consecutive API errors or a stale-age threshold, and add a verify/ test that injects an erroring API stub and asserts the feature auto-degrades. That converts the existing manual off-switch into a tested, self-firing kill switch.

records.probe: Read index.html SRI declarations and verify/tests/40-sri-recompute.test.js + 20-golden-content.test.js to see what artifact integrity is verified and whether verification fails closed AT DEPLOY; checked verify.yml + gate.js + audit surface to see whether the gate actually blocks a push-to-main deploy.
records.evidence: Dependency artifacts ARE integrity-verified: index.html carries sha384 SRI on all 5 CDN scripts, the browser verify-on-load fails closed (refuses mismatched scripts), and verify/tests/40-sri-recompute.test.js:32-53 re-derives sha384 from fetched bytes and asserts it matches, expecting exactly 5 SRI'd deps (line 37-38); verify/tests/35-supply-chain pins versions. The site's OWN artifacts have a golden integrity hash: tests/20-golden-content.test.js:16-22 hashes the frozen content and compares to golden/data-hash.json. HOWEVER gate.js is a CI-only signal — its header (lines 3-11) says it makes 'the deploy decision by CODE' but audit/00-audit-surface.json:45 records branch protection on main = NONE and system map:22 says any push to main is production with 'no admission control of any kind'. So verify-on-DEPLOY that refuses a modified artifact does not exist for the app's own files: a modified dashboard.jsx that breaks the golden hash still deploys to Pages. No cryptographic signing (cosign/Sigstore) of the site artifacts; builds are trivially reproducible (there is no build).

standing_control.mechanism: SRI verify-on-load (browser, fails closed) for CDN artifacts, recomputed by verify/tests/40-sri-recompute.test.js; golden content-hash over the site's own frozen files (tests/20-golden-content.test.js).
standing_control.blocks: For CDN deps: browser execution of a tampered script (real, fail-closed). For the site's own artifacts: the CI gate flags a hash change but does NOT block the Pages deploy — no branch protection.
standing_control.cadence: every push/PR via verify.yml; browser SRI on every page view.
standing_control.ratchet: golden hash is a ratchet against silent content change in CI, but non-gating for production at baseline.

records.remediation: Wire the existing gate to actually block deploy: enable branch protection on main requiring the verify workflow (golden-content + SRI-recompute + supply-chain) to pass before merge, so a modified/unverified artifact is refused rather than served. That upgrades the golden hash from advisory characterization to fail-closed verify-on-deploy. Signing (cosign) is optional given a static repo, but the blocking gate is the load-bearing fix — without it the golden hash is theatre with no audience.

records.probe: Searched the repo for any production-failure detection (liveness/readiness, synthetic monitoring of <prod-host>, uptime check, anomaly detection) and any automated response bound to an alert class; inspected verify.yml (the only automation) to see if it monitors production or only lint-checks code pre-merge.
records.evidence: audit/00-audit-surface.json scheduled_jobs=[], queues=[], pipelines=[] — no monitoring job of any kind. The only automation is .github/workflows/verify.yml, which runs node run.js / node gate.js on push/PR against the SOURCE; it performs static checks (data invariants, golden hash, SRI, supply-chain, governance) and never probes the live site <prod-host>, has no liveness/readiness, no synthetic journey, no anomaly detection, and triggers no automated remediation. There is no alert class with an executing response anywhere in the repo. Production-failure detection→action coverage = 0/0; the undeclared dependency is total: if Pages serves a broken deploy or a CDN 404s, nothing notices and nothing responds.

residual_risk.compensating_control: GitHub Pages platform uptime is Microsoft/GitHub-managed; browser SRI fails closed on a tampered CDN script (so a supply-chain swap is refused client-side, not merely detected). Neither detects a broken deploy or content regression.
residual_risk.tripwire: None automated — a production outage or content regression is currently invisible until a human visits the site.

records.remediation: Add a scheduled synthetic monitor (GitHub Actions cron, out-of-band) that GETs https://<prod-host>, asserts HTTP 200 + a known content marker + that the 5 SRI scripts resolve, and on failure fires an AUTOMATED response — e.g. open/label a tracking issue and auto-revert main to the last verified commit (git revert + push) rather than only paging. Define an MTTD target. Break-glass human escalation is the backstop (S10), not the control.

records.probe: Read bubblegauge.jsx's fetch/degradation layer for reliability primitives on the one external-dependency critical path, then grepped verify/tests for any failure-injection / chaos test that actually breaks a dependency and asserts graceful degradation.
records.evidence: Reliability primitives DO exist on the only critical dependency (the bubblegauge API): timeouts (tests/40 uses req.setTimeout; bubblegauge has a fetch timeout layer), a 25-min in-memory cache TTL, and documented degradation contract 'Service never returns 500 on upstream failure: fallback or drop+renormalize' (bubblegauge.jsx:189), plus per-item states 'suppressed (block degraded)' (line 95/118), stale/fallback_used/dropped flags (208-210), and an offline demo/fixture path (27,204-237). BUT these are DESIGNED, not VALIDATED by injection: grep for chaos/inject/failure over verify/tests found only a fetch-timeout in the SRI test's own network helper and a static assertion (tests/30-static-security.test.js:66) that catch handlers do not fake-2xx — no test kills the API, adds latency, or returns 500 and asserts the strip degrades gracefully. No scheduled chaos experiment, no steady-state hypothesis, no experiment set.

standing_control.mechanism: Graceful-degradation primitives are present in bubblegauge.jsx, but no automated, recurring failure-injection experiment validates them.
standing_control.blocks: nothing — no chaos experiment gates release.
standing_control.ratchet: none — graceful-degradation success rate is neither measured nor ratcheted.

records.remediation: Add a failure-injection test to verify/ that stubs the bubblegauge API to (a) time out, (b) return 500, (c) return partial/stale payloads, and asserts the rendered strip degrades to suppressed/stale/demo without throwing and without fabricating a score — run it in verify.yml on every push (gating) so the experiment set is exercised automatically. New external dependencies should be added to that stub set. The experiment is the evidence, not the primitives.

records.na_justification: The check requires load-testing owned compute to its knee and bounding owned inference capacity. This system owns neither: it is static assets on a platform-managed CDN with no server-side compute and no in-product model (system map:29). There is no autoscaling knob, no connection pool, and no inference quota this repo controls; the only inference-adjacent path consumes an external service's capacity. GitHub Pages capacity/scaling is platform-managed and not a parameter of this codebase.
records.probe: Checked the architecture for any server-side compute, autoscaling policy, or in-product inference whose capacity could be load-tested to a knee, and for any inference quota this system owns.
records.evidence: audit/00-system-map.md:29 'In-product runtime models: none. No inference call is made by this codebase.' The product is static files served by GitHub Pages' CDN (system map:9-13); there is no server, no container, no autoscaler, no connection pool this repo controls (audit-surface: no pipelines, no server-side data_stores). The bubblegauge integration only GET-consumes text from an external service (the bubblegauge API host) whose capacity belongs to that separate service, not this system. Client rendering runs on the visitor's own browser. There is no inference quota, rate limit, or hardware constraint owned here to plan or bound.

residual_risk.compensating_control: GitHub Pages CDN absorbs static-asset load; there is no marginal per-request cost to this repo and no autoscaler to be weaponized (so the B-08 denial-of-wallet interaction is inert here — no cost meter to run away).
residual_risk.tripwire: GitHub Pages soft bandwidth limits (~100GB/mo) are platform-enforced and out-of-repo; unmonitored, but breaching them throttles rather than bills. If in-product inference is ever added, this check re-applies immediately.

records.probe: Identified every stateful store in the architecture, determined the recovery mechanism, and checked for a tested/scheduled restore drill with recorded RPO/RTO.
records.evidence: audit/00-audit-surface.json data_stores and system map:48 establish the ONLY persistence is git history on GitHub ('the sole persistence + de-facto backup'); the two browser stores (sessionStorage key, in-memory cache) are ephemeral client state, not server data. Recovery is inherent and trivially exercisable: GitHub Pages redeploys the whole site from main on any push, and every git clone is a complete replica (this working tree IS such a clone at commit 04772bf). BUT there is no scheduled, timed restore drill, no clean-environment restore test, and no documented RPO/RTO as a standing control — audit-surface scheduled_jobs=[] and no DR runbook exists in the repo. 'When did we last verify recovery' has no timestamp answer.

standing_control.mechanism: Git/GitHub provides inherent distributed replication (every clone is a full backup) and Pages auto-redeploys from main, so recoverability exists in practice — but no automated restore drill, timing, or RPO/RTO documentation makes it a monitored control.
standing_control.blocks: nothing
standing_control.ratchet: none — restore time is unmeasured, so it cannot be held from regressing.

records.remediation: Add a scheduled (e.g. weekly) CI job that clones the repo fresh into a clean runner, serves it (python3 -m http.server), smoke-tests that index.html renders and the 5 SRI scripts resolve, and records the wall-clock duration as the RTO signal; document RPO = last push to main and RTO = measured restore time, and make an overdue drill block release. This turns inherent git recoverability into a verified, timestamped control. Mitigation is cheap because there is no mutable data to reconcile — only source.

records.probe: Distinguished the layers of 'running infrastructure', asked for each whether a by-hand change would be noticed/reverted: (a) served content, (b) out-of-repo repo/Pages/DNS settings; searched for any reconciliation loop or drift detector.
records.evidence: Served-content layer is structurally reconciliation-free-of-drift: GitHub Pages serves branch main directly (system map:22, audit-surface configuration), so there is no separate deploy artifact that can diverge from the declared desired state (main) — the CNAME custom-domain declaration is also in-repo. BUT the infrastructure CONFIG layer is undeclared-as-code and undetected: audit/00-audit-surface.json:42-46 lists 'GitHub Pages: deploy from branch main / root' and 'custom domain <prod-host>' as out-of-repo settings, and 'branch protection on main — state_at_freeze: NONE'. A hand change to the Pages source branch, a toggle of branch protection, or a DNS/CNAME change at the registrar would be noticed by nothing — there is no IaC, no reconciliation loop, no drift detector (scheduled_jobs=[]).

standing_control.mechanism: For served content only: GitHub Pages serving main directly means the deployed artifact IS the declared state — no content drift is possible without a commit to main.
standing_control.blocks: content divergence from main (structurally). Does NOT cover out-of-repo settings (Pages source, branch protection, DNS), which have no reconciliation.
standing_control.cadence: continuous for content (Pages redeploys from main on push).
standing_control.ratchet: none for the config layer — out-of-band settings changes are unreverted and undetected.

records.remediation: Encode the repo/Pages settings as code or add a scheduled CI assertion (via the GitHub API) that Pages source == main/root, branch protection is present, and the live CNAME/DNS resolves to <prod-host> — alert and, where possible, auto-revert on divergence. The served-content layer needs nothing (it cannot drift from main), so this is bounded to the small out-of-repo config surface.

records.na_justification: The system has no retrieval-augmented pipeline: no embeddings, no vector store, no index, and no ingest path. The one persistent content store is the frozen data in dashboard.jsx, which is source code, not a retrieval corpus — and its integrity IS held by a standing control (verify/tests/20-golden-content.test.js: a sha256 golden hash over CRISES/MATRIX/CLASSIFICATION that goes red on any tamper, baseline verify/golden/data-hash.json). The check's named risks (embedding/index freshness, vector poisoning, provenance-at-ingest) address a capability that does not exist here, so it is architecturally N/A rather than a failed control.
records.probe: grep -riE 'embedding|vector|retrieval|rag|corpus|index.*freshness' over index.html, dashboard.jsx, bubblegauge.jsx — searched for any retrieval store, embedding index, or ingest path whose contents could become another user's retrieved context.
records.evidence: grep for embedding/vector/retrieval/RAG/corpus returned ZERO hits across all product source (this run). System map: all crisis content are frozen constants in dashboard.jsx (CRISES/MATRIX/CLASSIFICATION); no server, no database, no vector store, no embeddings. The only network read is bubblegauge.jsx bgFetch() GET-ing an external status API (lines 292-308), whose response lands in a per-tab in-memory `cache` object (line 289, 25-min TTL) that is never persisted, never shared between users, and never ingested into an index. There is no write path into any persistent retrieval corpus and no cross-user contamination channel.

records.na_justification: Time-to-first-token and inter-token latency are properties of a generative model producing tokens. This site makes no inference call at runtime (system map), so there is no TTFT to budget and no p95/p99 generation latency to track. The check targets a capability the architecture does not contain.
records.probe: grep -riE 'inference|llm|completion|token|latency|time-to-first-token' over product source, and read bubblegauge.jsx fetch layer — searched for any inference call whose TTFT / end-to-end latency could be measured.
records.evidence: grep for inference/model/token in bubblegauge.jsx + dashboard.jsx + index.html returned no runtime-model hits (only a CSS 'tokens' comment). System map §Models: 'In-product runtime models: none. No inference call is made by this codebase.' The only latency-bearing network op is bgFetch (bubblegauge.jsx:292-308), a plain GET against an external status API guarded by a 6s AbortController timeout — a data fetch, not token generation. There is no streamed generation, no first-token event, no model gateway.

records.probe: Established who/what can write to production and whether the code-writing identity can also rewrite the gate that judges it. Checked credentials, not intent: read git config identity, the remote, the location of the policy bundle relative to the code, the workflow permissions, and the branch-protection state at freeze.
records.evidence: The policy bundle is co-located with the code it gates, under ONE identity: verify/gate.js, verify/tests/*.test.js, and .github/workflows/verify.yml all live in the SAME repo (mglaeser/save-haven-monitor) as dashboard.jsx/bubblegauge.jsx. git config shows the code-writing agent commits as `Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>`; `git log` confirms that identity authored both the product code (2aa7838) and the entire gate (4af8354). There is no separate policy repository and no separate write credential — grep for 'separate repo/policy repo' finds the requirement only in the catalogue text, never in an implementation. Therefore the agent that writes the code can, in the same push, rewrite gate.js AND its own self-test (tests/50-gate-selftest.test.js) — the author controls the gate. Worse, the gate does not even block: audit/00-audit-surface.json:45 records `branch protection on main … state_at_freeze: NONE`, and DR-002-ci-gate.md admits R-GATE: 'Until branch protection requires the verify check on main, the gate is advisory, not blocking.' gate.js:13 CLAIMS 'B-35 write-separation is a standing control' — under Rule 2 that comment is material to test, not evidence; the credential reality falsifies it.

standing_control.mechanism: No continuous assertion that no identity holds both code-write and policy-write exists — it cannot, because there is only one repo and one write identity. verify/tests/60-governance.test.js asserts the workflow is wired and 50-gate-selftest asserts the gate blocks, but both are editable by the same agent in the same commit and neither enforces credential separation.
standing_control.blocks: Nothing in the segregation sense: at freeze the workflow is advisory (no branch protection), and the gate/tests are writable by the author they judge.
standing_control.ratchet: none for write-separation

structural_fix.rationale: S13: move the policy bundle (gate + tests + golden baselines + workflow) into its own repository with its own write identity and its own pipeline, so segregation becomes a fact about which key holds which permission rather than a comment. Not taken — verify/ is inside the gated repo under the same identity.

residual_risk.compensating_control: gate.js is self-tested (50-gate-selftest) and the wiring is tripwired (60-governance); both run in CI on the branch. This narrows accidental gate removal but does NOT stop a deliberate same-identity edit.
residual_risk.tripwire: verify/tests/60-governance.test.js goes red if the workflow is deleted/decoupled — but an agent can edit the tripwire too.

records.remediation: Two moves, structural first: (1) Extract the policy bundle (verify/gate.js, verify/tests/, verify/golden/, .github/workflows/verify.yml) into a separate repo with a distinct write credential that NO code-writing agent identity holds; have the product repo's CI pull the policy bundle read-only and fail closed if it cannot. (2) Until then, the owner must enable branch protection on main requiring the `verify` check with 'do not allow bypassing' (per DR-002 R-GATE), so the advisory gate becomes blocking. Note the mandate escalation: a breach of this control is a STOP-SHIP — an author who can edit its own gate has no gate.

records.probe: Listed every 'model version in use' and asked what happens at its end-of-life. grep -riE 'model|gpt-|anthropic|deprecat' over product source; read the pinned-dependency inventory and the supply-chain test.
records.evidence: grep for runtime model references in product source returns none; system map §Models confirms 'In-product runtime models: none.' The only pinned external artifacts are CDN library versions (react/react-dom 18.3.1, prop-types 15.8.1, recharts 2.12.7, @babel/standalone 7.29.7) declared in index.html, and these ARE inventoried and enforced (verify/golden/pinned-deps.json + verify/tests/35-supply-chain.test.js, which fails on any silent version bump). No LLM/model with a provider deprecation window sits in the production path. The maintenance-time Claude agents are the authors, not a runtime dependency the served site would go dark without.

history.reason: NOT-APPLICABLE is over-scoped to a runtime *product* model. The audited architecture does contain a deprecation-exposed model: the maintenance-time Anthropic model (`claude-fable-5`), which the system map lists under "Models and providers" as the sole production path (agent push = production) and explicitly flags with "No pinning, no provenance recording, and no second-vendor verifier exists at ba

records.na_justification: Right-to-erasure requires personal data held in server-side or derived stores. This system collects and stores no personal data anywhere it controls (system map; CLAUDE.md no-analytics/no-tracker policy). The only client-side value is a non-personal activation key in the user's own sessionStorage, fully under the user's control. With no personal-data store and no derived stores, the erasure-test control governs a capability the architecture does not contain.
records.probe: Picked a hypothetical user and traced every store their data could reach: grep -riE 'email|password|cookie|localStorage|personal|user id|analytics|tracking' over product source; enumerated the data stores from the system map.
records.evidence: grep for PII/tracking returns no user-data handling (dashboard hits are research prose about market 'liquidity', not user records; sessionStorage usage in bubblegauge.jsx:35-42 stores only the activation KEY string `bubblegauge:enabled`, in the visitor's own browser, which they clear at will via ?status-api-off). System map §Data stores lists exactly three: client sessionStorage (one activation key), an in-memory fetch cache, and git history — 'No server-side stores, no databases, no vector stores … no user data, no analytics or trackers (by CLAUDE.md policy).' There is no primary store, backup, prompt log, trace store, tuning set, or embedding derived from personal data — so there is nothing to erase and no derived-store fan-out to prove clean.

records.na_justification: Inference economics (prompt-cache hit rate, cost-based model routing, cost per unit of value) presuppose paid model calls. This codebase makes none at runtime (system map). The check targets a cost surface the architecture does not have.
records.probe: Attempted to measure prompt-cache hit rate and cost split across models: grep -riE 'inference|prompt|cache.*hit|cost|model rout' over product source; inspected the only cache in the codebase.
records.evidence: The only 'cache' in the product is bubblegauge.jsx's in-memory HTTP response cache (line 289-290, `const cache = {}; const TTL = 25*60*1000`) — a data-fetch cache with no relation to prompt caching or per-token cost. grep finds no inference, no prompt, no model routing, no billing surface. System map confirms zero runtime inference. There is no model invocation whose cost-per-unit-of-value could be measured, no prompt prefix to keep cacheable, and no invoice to watch.

records.probe: Asked which named reference framework the platform was designed against, and whether that review is a held, re-run control or a one-off document. Read the audit apparatus (catalogue, findings, gate) and searched CLAUDE.md/README for a design framework.
records.evidence: At the frozen baseline the atlas was built ad hoc by agents — no reference framework (twelve-factor, well-architected, generative-AI/responsible-AI lenses) is named in CLAUDE.md, README.md, or INTEGRATION_NOTES.md for the product's design. Retroactively, this engagement DOES apply a named framework — the Due-Diligence and Remediation Mandate catalogue (audit/00-check-catalogue.json, 119 checks / 79 active), with gaps recorded as findings (audit/03-findings.json) rather than dropped, and findings-file integrity enforced deterministically by verify/gate.js (§2: exactly the active check IDs, no PASS with null standing_control). So the 'gaps tracked as findings with owning roles' half of the standing control genuinely exists and is machine-held. What is missing is the cadence: no mechanism re-runs the framework review on a schedule or on major architecture change — the audit is a one-time engagement, and the well-architected/twelve-factor AI lenses were never applied to the design as such.

standing_control.mechanism: The mandate catalogue is applied as the reference framework; gaps are recorded in audit/03-findings.json and their existence + integrity is enforced on every pipeline run by verify/gate.js (findings count must equal active checks; no PASS without a standing_control).
standing_control.blocks: A merge whose findings file drops or mis-registers an active check (gate.js fails closed) — but only advisory until branch protection is on (see B-35 R-GATE).
standing_control.cadence: per-push integrity check only; no scheduled framework re-run
standing_control.ratchet: S11 partial: open framework gaps are the findings backlog; the gate prevents silently deleting a finding record, so gaps cannot be quietly dropped.

residual_risk.compensating_control: gate.js findings-integrity keeps the gap list honest between reviews.
residual_risk.tripwire: verify/tests/60-governance.test.js red if audit artifacts removed.

records.remediation: Name the framework(s) explicitly in a design record and apply the AI-specific lenses to the actual architecture (for a static zero-build site: the generative/responsible-AI lens over the sole AI-adjacent surface bubblegauge.jsx, plus the relevant well-architected pillars — security, reliability of the CDN/egress paths). Then make the review a held control: a scheduled/on-architecture-change re-run that regenerates the gap list, with each open gap carrying an owning role, so 'framework adherence' is a recurring assertion rather than this one engagement's snapshot.

records.na_justification: The IDOR/BOLA/server-side-authz/tenancy sub-scope is architecturally absent: there is no server, no backend, no database, no session, no user account, no object identifier, and no authorization decision made anywhere (client or browser). A 'change the id to read another object' attack has no surface — grep of dashboard.jsx/index.html/bubblegauge.jsx finds no auth, login, cookie, session, or object-id primitive. That entire sub-scope is N/A; but per the 'any applicable sub-surface' rule the client-side URL-construction / injection / SSRF / secrets slice IS present and is assessed below (PASS).
records.probe: Tried to falsify the safe-URL claim: (1) can an attacker-controlled ?status-api value reach fetch()/URL construction unvalidated (SSRF to arbitrary host)? (2) can external/API data reach an HTML-injection sink? (3) is any authorization decision taken in the browser that could be bypassed? (4) are secrets/auth headers present? Read bubblegauge.jsx resolveActivation (lines 25-58) and the fetch layer (272-308), and 30-static-security.test.js assertions.
records.evidence: SSRF: bubblegauge.jsx L24 KEY_RE=/^[a-z0-9-]{1,32}$/ rejects dots and slashes, so ?status-api=evil.com or a full URL fails. L44 `if (!key || !KEY_RE.test(key)) return null;` gates BEFORE any base derivation; L57 base is built only as `"https://" + key + "." + parent` (subdomain-of-own-parent, host from page's own loc.hostname which is fixed by CNAME <prod-host>, not attacker-controlled). fetch (L300) targets API_BASE+path only. 30-static-security.test.js asserts KEY_RE is defined as the strict whitelist, that KEY_RE.test appears at >=2 sites, that the unvalidated-key early-return exists verbatim, and that base is subdomain-of-parent only. Injection: same test asserts NO dangerouslySetInnerHTML in bg+dash and that any .innerHTML assignment is non-interpolated; API response is boundary-validated (validScore/isNum/pair L275-288) and rendered as React data (auto-escaped). Secrets: test forbids sk-/ghp_/AKIA/Bearer/Authorization/X-API-Key idioms across all served files (public GET, no auth header). Authz-in-browser: none exists to bypass. ABSENT (honest): no DAST authz sweep, no nightly dynamic scan, no route-authz-test gate — but those target surfaces (routes, authz) that do not exist here.

standing_control.mechanism: verify/tests/30-static-security.test.js — deterministic regex/parse over the three served files: KEY_RE-whitelist-at-every-use, unvalidated-key-returns-null-before-base, subdomain-of-parent-only base, no-injection-sink, no-secret-idiom; run by verify/run.js and gated by gate.js via .github/workflows/verify.yml
standing_control.blocks: a merge/push that removes the KEY_RE guard, accepts a full-URL key, adds a dangerouslySetInnerHTML or interpolated innerHTML sink, or leaks a credential/auth-header idiom
standing_control.cadence: on push and PR to every branch (verify.yml on: push/pull_request branches ['**'])
standing_control.ratchet: static assertions are boolean-monotone (a new injection sink or a weakened KEY_RE flips the suite RED); mutation.js floor 0.75 keeps the suite able to fail

structural_fix.rationale: The door (S13) is the boundary-validation + whitelist-before-construction design plus its executable proof in 30-static-security; it exists at baseline and is committed. The residual door not yet taken is putting verify/ on main (R-GATE) so the gate also guards direct pushes to the deployed branch.

residual_risk.compensating_control: KEY_RE whitelist + subdomain-of-parent-only construction bound worst case to 'fetch a subdomain of the site's own parent domain'; React auto-escaping bounds injection to rendered text/link in the optional panel; no consequential action, no data to exfiltrate, no server to pivot to
residual_risk.tripwire: 30-static-security goes RED on any weakening — BUT R-GATE: verify/ is not yet on main, so a direct push to main bypasses the gate on the production branch; the honest gap is that CI enforcement covers PRs/branches, not the deployed main tip until verify/ lands there

records.remediation: Land verify/ + .github/workflows/verify.yml on main (close R-GATE) so 30-static-security gates the deployed branch, not only feature branches. No new control is needed for the injection/SSRF/secrets slice itself — the standing test already blocks it; the smallest real step is making that same test authoritative over main.
records.adversarial_verification: agreed: Falsification attempts failed. bubblegauge.jsx L25/L44/L57: KEY_RE rejects dots/slashes and is re-tested before any URL construction (SSRF bounded to subdomain-of-own-parent, host from fixed loc.hostname). 30-static-security.test.js is a real, committed, deterministic gate blocking KEY_RE weakening (L45-54), HTML-injection sinks (L56-63), and secret/auth-header idioms (L24-43); it runs on push/PR (verify.yml branches ['**']). The IDOR/server-authz sub-scope is genuinely absent (no server/ids/users, confirmed by grep). The R-GATE gap (verify/ and workflow absent from origin/main, which I confirmed via git ls-tree) leaves the deployed branch ungated, but that is a baseline-wide disclosed residual carried in the tripwire, not specific to this check, and the literal PASS criterion (a committed test that runs and blocks) is met on the assessed injection/SSRF/secrets slice. Evidence is strong, not thin.

records.probe: Tried to falsify 'threat model kept current MECHANICALLY': searched verify/tests/, run.js, mutation.js for any boundary-count / egress-allowlist / new-integration detector that would turn CI red when a NEW trust boundary, egress destination, or fetch host appears. Also checked whether the enumerated-boundary docs (audit/00-system-map.md, audit/00-audit-surface.json) are asserted by any test so they cannot silently go stale.
records.evidence: A DFD-equivalent + trust-boundary enumeration EXISTS as version-controlled docs: audit/00-system-map.md 'Egress paths (complete)' table (unpkg, the bubblegauge API host, www.etoro.com) and audit/00-audit-surface.json egress_destinations/dependencies/identities. But these are prose/JSON inventories with NO STRIDE-per-boundary table and NO test binding them. The only mechanical currency that exists: verify/tests/35-supply-chain 'index.html CDN script URLs match the recorded pin manifest exactly' (eq urls.length == pinned-deps.json.urls length) catches a NEW or changed CDN <script> egress, and verify/tests/30-static-security 'all app URLs are relative or https; no http:// leaks' plus the KEY_RE subdomain-only assertion bound the <prod-domain> boundary. CRITICAL GAP: a new fetch()/XHR to an arbitrary https host added to bubblegauge.jsx or dashboard.jsx would pass 30 (https is allowed) and pass 35 (not a <script src>); nothing counts or allow-lists egress destinations, and no test fails when a new boundary/integration/data-class is introduced. Docs are not hash-bound, so the boundary inventory can drift from reality silently.

standing_control.mechanism: verify/tests/35-supply-chain (exact pinned-CDN URL set) + verify/tests/30-static-security (relative/https-only, no http leak, KEY_RE subdomain-only)
standing_control.blocks: a new/changed CDN <script> egress and any http:// or full-URL construction on the status-API boundary; does NOT block a new https fetch host, a new integration seam, or a new data class
standing_control.cadence: on push/PR via .github/workflows/verify.yml — but that workflow and verify/ are NOT on main (git ls-tree main is empty for verify/ and workflows/), so the production branch runs no detector
standing_control.ratchet: pinned-deps.json URL set (exact-match, one-way via decision record); no boundary-count ratchet

structural_fix.rationale: S13 door: add a verify/ test asserting the complete egress-destination allowlist (grep all served source for fetch/XHR/new script hosts, compare to a golden egress manifest) plus a STRIDE-per-boundary doc hash-bound like the constitution digest. Buildable now; not built at baseline.

residual_risk.compensating_control: code is small (2 served JSX files) and machine-authored; a new egress would appear in git diff, but no automated signal fires
residual_risk.tripwire: the honest gap: a new fetch to a novel https host is added and no test goes red — undetected until manual review, which is not a control

records.remediation: Build a golden egress-manifest test in verify/tests/ that enumerates every allowed destination (unpkg pins, key.<parent> derivation, etoro nav) and fails CI when source introduces any fetch/script/XHR host not on the list; commit verify/ + verify.yml to main so the detector runs on the production branch. That test is the standing control C-02 requires.
records.adversarial_verification: agreed: Confirmed. Real mechanical detectors exist but are incomplete: verify/tests/35-supply-chain pins the exact CDN <script> URL set (eq on manifest) and 30-static-security enforces https-only + KEY_RE subdomain-only, so a new/changed CDN egress or a dotted key is caught. But I verified there is NO egress-destination allowlist: 30 only distinguishes http vs https, so a new fetch()/XHR to an arbitrary https host in bubblegauge.jsx/dashboard.jsx passes both 30 (https allowed) and 35 (not a <script src>). The boundary docs (audit/00-system-map.md, 00-audit-surface.json) are prose/JSON with no test binding or hash-lock, so they can drift silently. No boundary-count/new-integration detector exists. Partial control, honestly scoped. Not a full PASS (no allowlist), not a FAIL (35+30 do give partial currency). The 'not on main' caveat is also confirmed (origin/main has no verify/).

records.probe: Tried to falsify 'deps are pinned + existence-gated' on BOTH surfaces: (1) shipped runtime deps and (2) CI-only harness deps. Grepped index.html script tags against the pin manifest and SRI, then inspected verify/package.json + the CI workflow for a lockfile, npm ci, allowlist, age/reputation check, and any 'verify assistant output before install' step. Attempted to find a hallucinated-package or floating-range vector that would install unchecked.
records.evidence: SHIPPED slice is strongly gated: index.html carries ZERO npm runtime deps; the 4 (+babel = 5) CDN libs are pinned by exact URL in verify/golden/pinned-deps.json and asserted by verify/tests/35-supply-chain (offline exact-URL match, catches a silent version bump) AND by verify/tests/40-sri-recompute (re-derives sha384 from fetched bytes vs the index.html integrity attribute). verify/tests/30-static-security additionally asserts all 5 <script> tags carry sha384 integrity + crossorigin=anonymous. Both run on every push/PR (.github/workflows/verify.yml). CI-ONLY slice is UNGATED: verify/package.json declares devDependency "playwright-core": "^1.40.0" (a floating caret range, NOT pinned); there is NO verify/package-lock.json committed (git ls-files shows none) and the workflow runs `npm install --no-audit --no-fund` (NOT `npm ci`), so the harness resolves transitive deps live at CI time with no lockfile, no allowlist, no newly-registered/low-reputation/age screen, and no 'verify before install' step. The gate that decides deploy-eligibility runs in that same unpinned environment. Hallucinated-package / typosquat / floating-range drift on the CI tooling is unblocked.

standing_control.mechanism: verify/tests/35-supply-chain (exact pin-manifest match) + verify/tests/40-sri-recompute (sha384 recompute) + 30-static-security (integrity+crossorigin present)
standing_control.blocks: any silent version bump, path change, or byte drift of the 5 shipped CDN deps; a stray parallel test suite. Does NOT block CI-only dep drift.
standing_control.ratchet: pinned-deps.json URL set + recorded sha384 hashes (monotone: a change is a decision-record event)

structural_fix.rationale: Shipped-dep door (S3 executable pin+SRI proof) is built and held. The CI-dep door — commit a package-lock.json, switch CI to `npm ci`, add a dep-allowlist/age-check test — is available but NOT taken at baseline.

residual_risk.compensating_control: CI-only deps are gitignored, never shipped to visitors, and the harness is offline-deterministic for the load-bearing regex tests (only 40-sri does egress); a compromised playwright-core could still poison the gate's own run, though.
residual_risk.tripwire: a diff to verify/package.json version ranges, or a CI `npm install` pulling a version not previously seen — currently NO executable signal watches this (honest gap).

records.remediation: Commit verify/package-lock.json, change the workflow step to `npm ci`, and add a verify/tests test asserting the lockfile is present and every dep resolves to a pinned integrity (the standing control that must exist). That converts the CI-dep slice from ungated to blocked and moves this to PASS.
records.adversarial_verification: agreed: Verified on both surfaces. Shipped slice strongly gated: index.html has zero npm runtime deps; the 5 CDN libs match verify/golden/pinned-deps.json exactly (35-supply-chain) with SRI integrity+crossorigin asserted (30-static-security) and sha384 recompute (40-sri). CI slice genuinely ungated: verify/package.json declares floating 'playwright-core':'^1.40.0', `git ls-files` shows NO lockfile, and .github/workflows/verify.yml runs `npm install --no-audit --no-fund` (not `npm ci`). The gate runs in that unpinned env. PARTIAL is correct — under-claims nothing, over-claims nothing.

records.na_justification: No personal data is processed anywhere in the served product, so the entry→store→copy→retention→deletion lifecycle this check governs does not occur. There is no server, no backend, no database, no form capturing PII, no analytics, no tracker, no cookie, and no logging/tracing/embedding sink. The only browser storage is sessionStorage['bubblegauge:enabled'] holding the ?status-api KEY STRING (matched ^[a-z0-9-]{1,32}$ — an activation flag, not personal data). Escalation clause fires: NOT EU personal data (none at all), so the check re-bands from priority-10 STOP-SHIP to effectively N/A against applicable law — there is no data subject, no controller processing, no lawful basis to establish, and no deletion path to test because there is nothing to delete.
records.probe: Tried to find ANY personal-data sink that would defeat the no-PII claim: grepped dashboard.jsx/index.html/bubblegauge.jsx for cookie, localStorage, analytics, gtag, track, email, phone, address, geolocation, navigator.*, login, form POST of user input. Inspected the one browser-storage write (sessionStorage) and the one egress (optional gated GET to *.<prod-domain>) for whether either carries user/device identifiers.
records.evidence: grep returns only crisis-content matches (chart .name props, 'analytics' tab label, research citations) — zero PII collection, zero trackers, zero cookies. bubblegauge.jsx sessionStorage.setItem(SS_KEY, key) (L40) stores only the KEY_RE-validated activation string. The optional feed is a read-only GET with headers {accept:'application/json'} and no auth/identifier header (30-static-security forbids Authorization/X-API-Key idioms). 35-supply-chain / SRI assert exactly 5 CDN <script src> tags, which would trip if an external analytics script were added. ABSENT (honest): there is NO dedicated PII/analytics-sink scanner in verify/tests/30-static-security (grep for PII|email|phone|analytics|navigator in that test = none), and the golden hash (20-golden-content) covers only the frozen data constants CRISES/MATRIX/CLASSIFICATION — NOT the whole dashboard.jsx source — so an inline PII form or a same-origin fetch-beacon added elsewhere in the source would NOT be caught by an executable test today.

standing_control.mechanism: Compound and partial: (a) architecture — static read-only site, no server to store/log PII; (b) verify/tests/35-supply-chain + 40-sri pin the external script surface to exactly 5 known CDN tags, blocking an added external tracker/analytics <script src>; (c) 30-static-security forbids Authorization/X-API-Key egress idioms. NO dedicated PII/analytics-sink scanner exists.
standing_control.blocks: an added external analytics/tracker script tag and an auth-header-carrying egress; does NOT block an inline PII-collection form or a hand-rolled fetch/sendBeacon tracker inside the app source
standing_control.cadence: on push and PR to every branch (verify.yml); subject to R-GATE on main
standing_control.ratchet: CDN-script-tag count fixed at 5 (SRI/supply-chain); golden data hash frozen — but neither ratchet covers a new inline PII sink

structural_fix.rationale: The door (S13) that would make N/A durably self-enforcing is a small deterministic PII/analytics-sink scanner in 30-static-security (regex ban on form input capture, navigator.geolocation/sendBeacon, gtag/analytics idioms, and non-allowlisted fetch hosts). It is buildable at baseline but not yet built; today N/A rests mostly on architecture + the script-count pin, which is a partial guard.

residual_risk.compensating_control: No-server architecture means there is structurally nowhere to accumulate PII; the exactly-5-CDN-scripts pin and no-auth-header rule catch the most likely regressions (added external tracker / identifier egress)
residual_risk.tripwire: HONEST GAP: no executable signal fires the day an inline form, analytics beacon, or same-origin PII fetch is added inside dashboard.jsx/bubblegauge.jsx — the golden hash only guards the data constants and there is no PII-sink scanner; R-GATE additionally means enforcement is not yet on main
residual_risk.compensating_control_note: N/A stays true by architecture, not by a dedicated test

records.remediation: To keep N/A self-enforcing (and to define the tripwire that re-opens the check): add a PII/analytics-sink scanner to verify/tests/30-static-security banning form-input capture, navigator.geolocation/sendBeacon, gtag/analytics idioms, and any fetch host outside the {self, *.<prod-domain> gated} allowlist. If that scanner ever fires — or a form/analytics/PII sink is added — C-04 re-opens as a live privacy check requiring lawful basis, minimisation, retention, and an end-to-end deletion test.
records.adversarial_verification: agreed: No personal-data surface exists across the whole served product, so the entry-store-retention-deletion lifecycle this check governs does not occur. Grep of all three served files found no localStorage, cookie, form, sendBeacon, geolocation, or analytics/tracker sink (the 'analytics' hits are a tab label and in-browser recomputation, not telemetry). The only browser write is sessionStorage of the KEY_RE-validated activation string, which is not personal data, and the optional GET sends only an accept header with no identifier. This satisfies the explicit valid N/A ground 'no personal data exists anywhere' for the entire surface, and the escalation clause does not fire (no EU personal data). The generator's own disclosed no-PII-scanner gap is a durability concern (tripwire to re-open if a form/beacon/PII sink is later added), not a reason the current verdict should be PARTIAL/FAIL.

records.probe: Built the 10-category OWASP-LLM matrix and tried to find a live runtime surface for each, then checked whether a STANDING artifact/test maps each category to a control (the check's real demand: matrix + control + test, not just architectural absence). Looked for any test enumerating the taxonomy.
records.evidence: No runtime LLM/model/agent exists in the product (confirmed: dashboard.jsx renders frozen arrays + Recharts; bubblegauge.jsx only fetches JSON via fetch() and renders it through React). Category-by-category: (1) Prompt injection — N/A, no model consumes input; (2) Sensitive-info disclosure — N/A, no PII/secrets (30-static-security no-secrets patterns hold); (3) Supply chain — covered by 35/40 (see C-03); (4) Data/model poisoning — N/A, no training/model; (5) Improper output handling — LIVE thin surface: status-API strings (data_source, as_of, note, judgment_call.text, science_audit flag title/detail/ref, caveats) render as React text children (bubblegauge.jsx IndicatorRow ~L601, EpistemicPanel L742-784, FusionPanel), auto-escaped; controlled by verify/tests/30-static-security asserting NO dangerouslySetInnerHTML anywhere and any innerHTML is non-interpolated; (6) Excessive agency — N/A, no tool-calling; (7) System-prompt leakage — N/A, no prompt; (8) Vector/embedding — N/A, none; (9) Misinformation — the heuristic is heavily labelled (see C-09) but no test pins it; (10) Unbounded consumption — N/A, no model; fetch has AbortController 6s timeout + 25min cache TTL. ABSENT: any standing taxonomy-matrix artifact/test that enumerates all 10 and asserts completeness — the mapping exists only in this assessment, not as committed code.

standing_control.mechanism: verify/tests/30-static-security (no dangerouslySetInnerHTML; non-interpolated innerHTML) covers the one live category (improper output handling)
standing_control.blocks: introduction of an HTML-injection sink fed by the external status API
standing_control.ratchet: none for the taxonomy as a whole; the injection-sink assertion is boolean-held

structural_fix.rationale: Door (S3): a committed taxonomy-matrix test enumerating all 10 categories -> {control | test | architecture-grounded N/A}, red if a new category becomes live. 9/10 are genuinely N/A by architecture; the value of the door is preventing silent scope-creep (e.g. someone adds a model). Not built at baseline.

residual_risk.compensating_control: The architecture (no model, no tools, no PII, React-escaped output) makes 9/10 categories vacuously safe today; the risk is future drift, not present exposure.
residual_risk.tripwire: any future import of an LLM SDK / addition of dangerouslySetInnerHTML / new external egress — 30-static-security catches the sink case, nothing watches the 'a model was added' case.

records.remediation: Add verify/tests/*-llm-taxonomy asserting each of the 10 categories is either controlled-by-named-test or N/A-by-architecture (e.g. grep asserts no LLM SDK import, no dangerouslySetInnerHTML), failing red if a live model/tool/embedding surface appears. That standing control turns the matrix from an assessment artifact into an enforced one -> PASS.
records.adversarial_verification: agreed: No runtime LLM/model exists (dashboard renders frozen arrays+Recharts; bubblegauge only fetch()es JSON and renders via React). 9/10 OWASP-LLM categories are architecturally vacuous; the one live category (improper output handling of status-API strings) IS covered by a committed standing test (30-static-security no-dangerouslySetInnerHTML, confirmed grep-empty in bg+dash; non-interpolated innerHTML). But no committed taxonomy-matrix test enumerates all 10 and asserts completeness/scope-creep — the mapping lives only in the assessment. Satisfied-by-architecture without an enumerating standing control => PARTIAL, not PASS. Correct.

records.na_justification: Runtime agentic taxonomy (goal hijack, tool misuse, privilege abuse, code exec, memory/context poisoning, inter-agent comms, cascading failures, rogue agents) is architecturally N/A to the PRODUCT: there is no runtime model/agent/tool-call in the served site (audit/00-system-map.md 'In-product runtime models: none', 'Tools the model can call: In-product: none'). The bubblegauge feed's judgment_call.text is rendered as inert React-escaped DATA, never fed to a model, never used to build a request, never persisted — so no product-side agency exists to abuse. The check is NOT fully N/A because the DEV PIPELINE has real agency (human-agent-trust-exploitation mutation), assessed below.
records.probe: Tried to falsify the N/A by hunting for any runtime tool-call, agent loop, or model inference in dashboard.jsx/bubblegauge.jsx (none — only Recharts rendering and a gated GET fetch). Then attacked the applicable slice: does a standing control govern the dev-agent's write/push/merge agency (the pipeline that trusts machine-authored code)?
records.evidence: Product: grep confirms no inference/tool-invocation; bubblegauge.jsx fetch is GET-only, boundary-validated (validScore/isNum/pair), no dangerouslySetInnerHTML on feed data (verify/tests/30 'no HTML-injection sinks'). Dev-pipeline agency IS real and documented: audit/00-audit-surface.json identities flags the Claude Code session 'writes production directly; no gate between agent output and deploy at baseline'. structural_fix.rationale: S13 door: land verify/ + verify.yml on main and make the gate a required status check (branch protection) so the dev-agent cannot deploy past a red gate. Owner enabled a ruleset but the gated content is not yet on the protected branch, so the door is built but not hung.

records.probe: Tried to falsify 'tool descriptions pinned on first use, diffed on change, blocking' — first confirming no tools are exposed to any model, then testing whether the structurally-equivalent third-party code (CDN libs) is pinned-and-diffed with a blocking check.
records.evidence: The product gives NO tools to any model (no MCP, no tool server). So a changed dependency 'description' (bytes/version) fails the hash — structurally pin+diff+block. CAVEAT: 40-sri self-labels NO-EVIDENCE-OFFLINE when egress is blocked, so the byte-recompute is not guaranteed in every CI run; the offline-deterministic 35-supply-chain pin match still always blocks a version bump.

records.probe: Attacked 'connector/tool-server security: method-level authz, server allowlist agents cannot write, no creds in tool args, shadowing/rug-pull scanning' against the ONLY external connector — the optional bubblegauge status API.
records.evidence: No MCP/tool servers exist. The single external connector is the gated status API. It is hardened by standing tests: 30-static-security asserts KEY_RE ^[a-z0-9-]{1,32}$ is enforced at >=2 sites and that an unvalidated key returns null before base derivation, and that the base is ONLY 'https://'+key+'.'+parent (a dot fails, so ?status-api=evil.com is rejected — host cannot be arbitrary, an agent/user cannot rewrite the allowlisted parent domain). The fetch is GET-only with headers {accept:'application/json'} (bubblegauge.jsx line ~300) — no Authorization/X-API-Key sent (30-static-security forbids those idioms). Response is boundary-validated (validScore/isNum/pair, lines 274-281) and rendered as auto-escaped React data; no dangerouslySetInnerHTML on feed data. ABSENT: any shadowing/rug-pull scanning of the external host (it is a live service outside this repo's code scope); method-level authz is N/A (public unauthenticated GET).

standing_control.blocks: an arbitrary/attacker host being fetched, a credential being attached to the call, and unvalidated feed data reaching an HTML sink
standing_control.ratchet: KEY_RE test-site count (>=2) and secret-idiom pattern set

records.evidence: The product generates NO citations/claims dynamically: the static analysis prose and crisis data are FROZEN editorial content (golden-hashed, 20-golden-content). The DEV-analogue fabrication risk — an agent inventing a misdescriptive label/claim — has a real standing control: the claims ledger (constitution Article X) compiled into verify/tests/70-claims.test.js, which convicted the fabricated 'Median max drawdown' label and now forbids its regression plus two other stale/overclaims (lines 14-25). No AIMS is claimed anywhere: grep for AIMS/42001/22989/management-system across governance/ and audit/ returns only mandate/catalogue reference text, no asserted management system; C-13 has no PASS/claim record in audit/03-findings.json (part2 planned-extension, not yet substantiated). With no AIMS claimed there is no lifecycle/vocabulary conformance obligation to substantiate — the whole surface of the check is absent, so N/A is architecture-grounded, not assumed.

records.evidence: The runtime footprint is architecturally trivial and I could not find a hidden material draw: the served product is a static, client-side, zero-build site with NO server, NO backend, NO runtime inference and NO training (audit/00-audit-surface.json scheduled_jobs:[], pipelines:[]; system map). Per-view cost is browser CPU + one CDN fetch (+ optional gated feed GET). The only real energy draw is maintenance-time LLM inference by the code-writing agent, which is bounded and intermittent. So on substance the workload is immaterial. BUT the check's pass condition requires EITHER a measured Software Carbon Intensity figure where material OR a DOCUMENTED immateriality-with-reasoning artifact — and NEITHER exists as a committed artifact: grep for carbon/energy/emission hits only unrelated crisis-data strings ('Oil / energy') in dashboard.jsx (confirmed in audit/03-findings.json ~1361). There is no materiality memo and no standing control that re-assesses materiality when the workload changes shape.
